_README: Agnostic kit template for the REFERENCE subsystem. Deployers MUST fill in `reference_sources` with the absolute paths to their own reference libraries (statutes, regulatory codes, standards, technical specs, doctrine, etc.). Leave `denylist_slugs` and `skip_file_patterns` updated for the project; the reference DB ingests AGNOSTIC content only -- project-specific facts belong in the evidence layer.
_doc_ops_log: The operations log (`ops_log_path`) is engineering bookkeeping for the reference pipeline -- source pulls, DB rebuilds, verifications, dev corrections. It carries no record-integrity weight. The evidence corpus's separate record-integrity trail (covering correspondence, documents, and ingested records) lives in `system/chain-of-custody.log` and is written by `lib_provenance.append_chain_of_custody()`; reference-pipeline tools must use `lib_provenance.append_operations_log()` instead.
_doc_reference_sources: Each entry is {slug, path, description}. `slug` becomes the subfolder name under reference/doctrine/. `path` is an absolute filesystem path to the source directory. The ingester walks each path looking for .md files. Empty list = nothing is ingested.
_doc_denylist_slugs: Slugs whose directory MUST NEVER be ingested even if accidentally added to reference_sources. Defense in depth.
_doc_skip_file_patterns: Filename substrings that mark a file as project-specific; even if it lives in an allowlisted subdir, it is skipped. Lowercase substring match against the full relative path.
_doc_skip_frontmatter_status: If a file's YAML frontmatter has `status: <value>` matching any entry here, the file is skipped at ingest. Common: 'stub' for incomplete drafts.
_doc_separation: BELT-AND-SUSPENDERS: tools/query_reference.py prefixes every row with [REFERENCE]. The two SQLite DBs (system/corpus.sqlite + system/reference.sqlite) share no tables, no triggers, no FTS namespace, and the query CLIs never JOIN across DBs.
